input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

delta_p.trigger: Apply when (a) the current output or draft explicitly states that the requested fact (here, a coach's middle name) is not present in the supplied passages (phrases like "not provided", "cannot be determined", "not available"), OR (b) the passages include a named head coach or equivalent bridge entity (presence of phrases like "head coach" plus a person name, e.g. "John Calipari").
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing behavior of extracting and summarizing the first-hop evidence that links the question to a bridge entity (the named coach) and retain any correct identification already present in the source summary (e.g., that the 1992–93 page names John Calipari as head coach).
delta_p.change_rule: Instead of stopping with a conclusion that the answer cannot be determined, produce a concise, action-oriented first-hop summary with three parts: (1) 'First-hop evidence:' identify which supplied passage/title names the coach and state that it establishes the coach's identity (cite the passage title exactly as it appears in the provided passages list); (2) 'Bridge entity / missing fact:' state the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the coach's full name as given there) and explicitly state which specific fact is missing from the supplied passages (the coach's middle name); (3) 'Retrieval direction for next hop:' give a short, concrete retrieval instruction recommending checking authoritative biographical sources (encyclopedic biography pages, official team/athletics bios, or reliable sports-reference profiles) and specify to inspect the lead/infobox or full-name/birth-name fields for the middle name. Do not attempt to guess or supply the missing middle name.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assert or invent the middle name; do not terminate with a final answer or a refusal. Avoid directing the next agent to informal sources (forums, reviews, or opinion pages) or to broad, unfocused searches. Avoid adding extra unrelated facts or long quoted spans from the passages.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce the single field 'summary' (preserving the agent's existing output interface). The 'summary' must be 2–4 short sentences or bullet-like lines following the three-part structure above, include the exact passage title that supplies the first-hop evidence, include the coach's name exactly as it appears in the passages, and end with a clear, short retrieval direction naming the type of biography page and the specific place to look (lead/infobox or full-name field). Do not include the middle name or any other new factual claim not present in the supplied passages.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The agent previously stopped with an endpoint statement that the middle name was not available. For the multi-hop pipeline, the next agent needs a clear bridge entity and an explicit retrieval direction. This prompt edit minimally redirects the agent from making a final 'cannot determine' claim to producing a structured, actionable first-hop summary that preserves the identified evidence while enabling a focused next-hop retrieval.

delta_p.trigger: Apply when the question requests a person's middle name or full given name (e.g., asks for a coach's middle name) and the supplied passages name that person but do not include the full/given-name field.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the agent's existing behavior of concisely summarizing the first-hop evidence and identifying the bridge entity found in the passages (use the name exactly as it appears in the passages).
delta_p.change_rule: Additionally, append a short, explicit 'Retrieval direction' instruction that (1) repeats the bridge entity name as found in the passages, (2) names recommended authoritative sources to consult (for example: the subject's biography/Wikipedia page, Sports-Reference profile, or official athletics bio), and (3) tells the next hop to check the lead/infobox or the 'birth_name'/'full_name'/'full given name' fields. Also provide a concise search-query template using the entity (example template: 'John Calipari full name middle name biography'). Do not attempt to answer the question or guess the middle name.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not invent or assert the person's middle name, do not claim the information is irretrievable without offering the retrieval direction, and avoid copying long verbatim spans from the passages as the summary.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce a brief 'summary' (2–3 short sentences): (a) one sentence naming the passage that supplies the first-hop evidence and the identified bridge entity (use the exact name from the passages), (b) one sentence stating the specific missing attribute needed (the person's middle/full given name), and (c) one short sentence labeled 'Retrieval direction' giving the recommended sources to check, the specific fields to inspect (lead/infobox, birth_name/full_name), and a one-line search-query template containing the bridge entity. Do not provide the middle name itself.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This small prompt edit turns a generic missing-fact notice into a concrete, actionable next-hop summary: it preserves the evidence summary while adding explicit, safe retrieval guidance so the pipeline's next agent can fetch the correct biographical field.

visible_agent_inputs.question: What is the middle name of the coach of UMass Minutemen basketball team in the 1992-93 season ?
passages: 1992–93 UMass Minutemen basketball team | The 1992–93 UMass Minutemen basketball team represented the University of Massachusetts Amherst during the 1992–93 NCAA Division I men's basketball season.  The Minutemen, led by fifth year head coach John Calipari were members of the Atlantic 10 Conference.  They finished the season 24-7, 11-3 in A-10 play to finish in first place.  It also marked the last season home games would be regularly played at Curry Hicks Cage.

1992 UMass Minutemen football team | The 1992 UMass Minutemen football team represented the University of Massachusetts Amherst in the 1992 NCAA Division I-AA football season as a member of the Yankee Conference.  The team was coached by Mike Hodges and played its home games at Warren McGuirk Alumni Stadium in Hadley, Massachusetts.  The 1992 season was Hodges' first as head coach of the Minutemen.  UMass finished the season with a record of 7–3 overall and 5–3 in conference play.

1999–2000 UMass Minutemen basketball team | The 1999–00 UMass Minutemen basketball team represented the University of Massachusetts Amherst during the 1999–00 NCAA Division I men's basketball season.  The Minutemen, led by fourth year head coach Bruiser Flint, played their home games at William D. Mullins Memorial Center and are members of the Atlantic 10 Conference.  They finished the season 17-16, 9-7 in A-10 play to finish in third place.

2008–09 UMass Minutemen basketball team | The 2008–09 UMass Minutemen basketball team represented the University of Massachusetts Amherst during the 2008–09 NCAA Division I men's basketball season.  The Minutemen, led by first year head coach Derek Kellogg, played their home games at William D. Mullins Memorial Center and are members of the Atlantic 10 Conference.  They finished the season 12-18, 7-9 in A-10 play to finish for tenth place.

2017–18 UMass Minutemen basketball team | The 2017–18 UMass Minutemen basketball team will represent the University of Massachusetts Amherst during the 2017–18 NCAA Division I men's basketball season.  The Minutemen, will be led by first-year head coach Matt McCall and will play their home games at the William D. Mullins Memorial Center in Amherst, Massachusetts as members of the Atlantic 10 Conference.

2009–10 UMass Minutemen basketball team | The 2009–10 UMass Minutemen basketball team represented the University of Massachusetts Amherst during the 2009–10 NCAA Division I men's basketball season.  The Minutemen were led by second year head coach Derek Kellogg and played their home games at William D. Mullins Memorial Center.  They were members of the Atlantic 10 Conference.  They finished the season 12–20, 5–11 in A-10 play to finish in 11th place.  The Minutemen lost to Richmond in the A-10 Tournament.

1997–98 UMass Minutemen basketball team | The 1997–98 UMass Minutemen basketball team represented the University of Massachusetts Amherst during the 1997–98 NCAA Division I men's basketball season.  The Minutemen, led by second year head coach Bruiser Flint, played their home games at William D. Mullins Memorial Center and are members of the Atlantic 10 Conference.  They finished the season 21–11, 12–4 in A-10 play to finish in third place.
visible_agent_inputs.current_output: The coach was John Calipari; his middle name is not provided in the supplied passages, so it cannot be determined from these sources.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.
